message:
refactor(writing/wills): attorney-optional framing, pricing refresh, viz layout, reader accuracy

Comprehensive update to components/wills-estate-article.tsx.

**Attorney-optional framing (primary conceptual shift)**
- Reframe the skill as delivering the expert analysis itself, not just
  intake prep. The skill produces a complete, signable will at the
  quality level of a white-shoe firm's wills-and-trusts division.
- Attorney section retitled "Using an attorney is strictly optional"
  (was "The attorney still signs the documents") and rewritten to put
  the expertise in the skill and frame attorney review as one option.
- FAQ rewrites:
  * "Is this legal advice?" -> "Not in the formal legal sense..."
  * "Do I still need an attorney?" -> "Not strictly..."
  * "Can my attorney see what the skill produced?" renamed to "Can I
    share what the skill produced with an attorney?"
  * Attorney-client-privilege FAQ reframed so the skill produces the
    plan and you execute under state formalities.
- Pattern-closing paragraphs rewritten to make the stronger claim
  clear: a frontier model + agent harness + the right skill can fully
  replace the specialist in many cases.
- TL;DR: "Your attorney still signs" -> "Attorney optional".
- TOC link, amber "licensed attorney still needs to review" warning
  removed; session-dialogue examples updated to not assume attorney
  involvement; tips paragraph softened; privacy FAQ softened.

**Pricing / setup refresh**
- GPT Pro in pricing table and setup step 1 updated to $100/mo per new
  OpenAI tier (was $200/mo).
- "Pick one" paragraph replaced with "The recommended setup is both":
  Claude Code (Opus 4.7 at maximum thinking-effort) for initial deep
  work, then Codex (GPT-5.4 at xhigh reasoning) to audit.
- New paragraph with exact commands: claude --model opus --effort max,
  codex --model gpt-5.4 -c model_reasoning_effort=xhigh, plus
  persistent config entries for settings.json and ~/.codex/config.toml.
- Download URL constants updated to claude.com/download and
  chatgpt.com/codex.

**Reader-accuracy and content corrections**
- "A few months ago I posted" -> "I recently posted" (skill shipped
  2026-04-14).
- "The replies kept pointing at the same one" -> "One reply suggested
  writing a will, and I knew right away it was the right answer."
- Subtitle's finished-will description rewritten (incredibly
  thoughtful, well-informed, custom-tailored to specific situation).
- Agent definition rewritten to name the actual tools (read files,
  edit files, save files, run code, open a browser) instead of vague
  "permission to do things".
- Document-formats note added: PDFs, MS Word, photos/scans of paper.
- Step-4 caveat added: the jsm.md skill link only works with an active
  subscription from step 3; otherwise it bounces to the login screen.
- Frontier-subscription paragraph replaced with concrete pointers to
  the tax-preparation skill (X post linked), modes-of-reasoning skill
  (X post linked), and 100+ other skills on jeffreys-skills.md.

**Duplicate button and visualization layout wrapper**
- Removed duplicate <MarkdownDownloadButton /> that appeared twice on
  the page; kept only the framed CTA in the download section.
- Wrapped each of the 7 interactive visualizations in a bleed-out
  container (w-full on mobile; at lg+ breakpoint: relative, left-1/2,
  -translate-x-1/2, w-[min(calc(100vw-48px),1200px)]) so that at
  >=1024px viewports they escape the 800px editorial column and
  render at up to 1200px centered. Verified via Playwright: parent
  width jumped from 752px to 1200px at 1440-wide viewport with zero
  horizontal overflow at mobile (390px).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

If you have the budget, run the - skill through both; the two frontier models catch different things, - and the two second opinions are the cheapest quality insurance - you'll buy this year. Cancel whichever ones you don't - want next month. You keep every document the skill produces - forever. + The recommended setup is both. Do the initial deep work in + Claude Code with Opus 4.7 at the maximum thinking-effort tier, + then hand the resulting package to Codex with GPT-5.4 at the + maximum reasoning-effort tier and ask it to audit what Claude + produced. The two frontier models do not overlap perfectly; a + gap one misses the other usually catches. About two hundred + and twenty dollars for a month of both is the cheapest + quality insurance you will buy this year, and you can cancel + either next month. If budget is tight, Claude Max alone is + enough to produce a real plan. You keep every document the + skill produces forever.

- The other thing worth saying about a hundred-dollar-a-month - frontier-model subscription: while you have it, it will also audit - your tax return, review your insurance coverage, draft a business - plan, compare Medicare plans, explain a confusing hospital bill, or - pre-read a long contract before you send it to a lawyer. It is - among the best hundred dollars per month you will spend this year - even before the estate plan. + Two short commands set the top reasoning tier on each tool. + Launch Claude Code with{" "} + claude --model opus --effort max, or inside a + session type /model opus and{" "} + /effort max. Launch Codex with{" "} + codex --model gpt-5.4 -c model_reasoning_effort=xhigh. + To make those the defaults, add{" "} + model: "opus" and{" "} + effortLevel: "max" to Claude Code's{" "} + settings.json, and add{" "} + model = "gpt-5.4" plus{" "} + model_reasoning_effort = "xhigh" to{" "} + ~/.codex/config.toml. These are the highest + reasoning tiers each tool exposes as of 2026.

- +

+ The other thing worth saying about a Claude Max or GPT Pro + subscription: the wills skill is one of a growing library on + the skills site. The same setup gives you access to a + world-class tax consultation for US residents (see{" "} + + my writeup on that one + + ), my modes-of-reasoning skill for pressure-testing any + project, business plan, or serious decision you are working + through (see{" "} + + this short post + + ), and more than a hundred other skills across different + specialist domains. The estate plan is one of several reasons + the subscription pays for itself in the first month. +

Your Medicare broker still enrolls - you. What changes is that a large share of the preparation can - happen across a couple of evenings or a weekend morning at your - kitchen table, for roughly a hundred and twenty dollars, instead - of entirely inside the specialist's billable hour. + That shape of problem is exactly what an agent-plus-skill + can compress and, in many cases, fully replace. Frontier + models in 2026 are that good. Agent harnesses are that + capable. Marry them with a skill that is comprehensive, + correct, well-conceived, and deliberately optimized for + agent use, and the combination can replace the specialist + outright across a surprising range of situations, at a + quality level that holds up against what a top firm would + produce. +

    + +

    + The caveat is the skill. A generic chatbot with a vague + prompt does not produce this result. The skill is where the + expertise is encoded: the frameworks a top firm uses + internally, the checklists, the decision trees, the edge + cases, the ugly special situations, the questions that + actually matter, the references that ground the reasoning. + Get that part right, and the frontier model plus agent + harness does the rest. They get the - signature. + This is the shape of a lot of what is coming. The older + view was that AI assists the professional. What is actually + happening, for a broad category of professional work that + is mostly careful intake, pattern-matching, and disciplined + drafting, is that AI running the right skill replaces the + professional entirely. For the cases where a licensed human + still has to sign something personally, you hand them a + finished, audited packet. For the many cases where no + signature is legally required, you skip that step and keep + the money.
